response: It depends on your plan. HMO plans require a referral from your primary care provider before we can see you, and we'll need it before your appointment or we may have to reschedule. PPO plans generally do not require one. If you're not sure, the number on the back of your insurance card can confirm it.
PPO plans generally let you see a specialist without a referral. HMO plans require a referral from your primary care provider before your visit. We'll ask which plan you have when we schedule you.

Dr. Shelby likes to do a consultation before the procedure. If it's been a few years since your last visit, or if you're having new symptoms, we'll schedule the consultation first and then schedule the procedure after you've spoken with him.
For cash-pay appointments, the consultation is $150. Procedure pricing depends on what's done, so please email the office and we'll get you an estimate.
We email you the pre-visit forms through PandaDoc before your appointment. You can fill them out completely online, and we receive your copy automatically once you're done.
After we schedule you, we'll send a text confirmation. Just reply to that text with photos of your insurance card, front and back, and we'll verify your coverage.
Yes — we send a text confirmation with the details of your appointment. You can reply to that text with photos of your insurance card, front and back.
Dr. Shelby generally performs procedures on Mondays and Wednesdays. The exact day varies by month, so we'll confirm when we schedule you.
Yes — Dr. Shelby offers phone appointments as well as in-person visits. Phone appointments often have earlier availability, especially in the afternoon. Which would you prefer?
A biopsy letter with your pathology results is mailed out about two weeks after your procedure. If you'd like to review the results with Dr. Shelby, we can schedule a follow-up.
